summary: End-to-end backend (19/19 pytest passing) + frontend UI smoke testing for the DocuSign MVP. All backend endpoints (auth, documents, signature requests, public signing with PDF embed, dashboard stats) work correctly. Frontend flows validated: register, login, dashboard KPIs, PDF upload + redirect, signature request creation, public sign page loads without auth. Public signing canvas submission could NOT be reliably simulated through Playwright due to a known limitation of react-signature-canvas/signature_pad (it ignores synthetic mouse/pointer events for its internal isEmpty/data state) — but the underlying signing pipeline including signed-PDF generation was fully verified via the backend tests.

minor.issue: FileResponse filename uses doc['title'] verbatim (e.g. with spaces/quotes) without sanitization — may break Content-Disposition for unusual titles. Not blocking.

integration_issues.flow: Public sign canvas submission via Playwright
integration_issues.issue: Cannot drive react-signature-canvas via synthetic mouse/pointer events (pixels render but signature_pad internal _data stays empty, so isEmpty() returns true and submit is short-circuited). Real users are unaffected; this only impacts automated UI tests. Recommend exposing a window-level test hook (e.g., window.__signFromDataURL) or accepting a hidden file input fallback for headless tests.

action_items: (Optional) Expose a test-only hook on PublicSign (e.g. window.__inksignSubmitSignature(dataUrl)) so e2e UI tests can submit a known PNG without simulating canvas drawing.
(Optional) Sanitize document title before using it as the Content-Disposition filename.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~450 lines and mixes auth, documents, signing and dashboard concerns — still acceptable for MVP, but consider splitting into routers for maintainability beyond this milestone.
Admin seed correctly resets password when env ADMIN_PASSWORD changes; cookies are httpOnly with samesite=lax; bcrypt hashes start with $2b$.
No brute-force lockout / rate-limiting on /api/auth/login — fine for MVP but worth noting.
embed_signature_into_pdf reads original PDF synchronously inside an async endpoint; OK for small files (20MB cap) but could be offloaded to a thread for larger files.
Public sign endpoint returns the full signature_request document (incl. owner_id) — minor info leak; consider projecting only needed fields.
test_credentials: admin@example.com / admin123 (seeded). New users created during tests use TEST_/ui_ prefixes.
seed_data_creation: Tests created several TEST_-prefixed users + documents + signature requests via the public API; not cleaned up but isolated by random suffix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers full E2E (auth, upload, list isolation, details, file download, signature request creation, public get + file + submit, dashboard stats). It uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and the tiny inline PDF + 1x1 base64 PNG signature. For frontend signing, if you need to verify it end-to-end via Playwright, add a window-level test hook on PublicSign or use the backend endpoint directly to flip the request to 'signed' and then verify the dashboard.
rca of the issue: react-signature-canvas wraps the signature_pad library which tracks stroke data via its own pointerdown/pointermove listeners and an internal _data array. Playwright synthetic mouse/pointer events draw pixels to the canvas but signature_pad's listeners do not always treat them as a real stroke (especially under devicePixelRatio scaling and headless contexts), so isEmpty() still returns true and the submit handler in PublicSign.jsx short-circuits with a 'Please draw your signature first' toast. Backend signing endpoint itself was verified directly and works correctly (request.status flips to 'signed', document.status flips to 'signed', signed PDF is generated and downloadable).
